title: Fresh Flaky Malaysian Dish Wrap
description: A kid-friendly, keto-friendly Malaysian dish featuring mirin, served in a pandan-leaf wrap.
instructions: Heat a small non-stick pan over medium heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and swirl it around.
Add the minced chicken and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spatula as it cooks.
Remove the chicken from the pan and set it aside.
In the same pan, add the grated carrot and cook until slightly tender, about 3 minutes.
Add the grated zucchini and cook for another 2 minutes, until slightly tender.
Add the sliced green chilies and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the cooked chicken back into the pan and stir to combine.
Add the furikake, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Crack an egg into the pan and scramble it with the chicken and vegetables.
Warm the pandan leaf wraps by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the wraps by spooning the chicken and vegetable mixture onto the center of each wrap, followed by chopped cilantro and a squeeze of fresh lime juice.
Fold the bottom edge of the wrap up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the wrap up tightly.
